content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's moves are tightly matched to live reads and sequenced correctly along the AoA path: feeling before action, refusing to strategize the external situation, and not validating the trap. Turn 2 drops into body rather than engaging the story. Turn 4 catches the laugh — a precise read of a tell. Turn 6 acknowledges the pain without colluding ('No, it doesn't change the org chart') and invites underneath. Turn 8 doesn't accept 'frustrated' as the final answer and offers a more accurate name ('heartbreaking') — a good 'I don't buy your story' move. Turn 10 explicitly names the move away from feeling rather than following it into logistics — refusing to strategize. Turn 12 catches 'idiot' as a sharper affect and goes after it. Turn 14 reframes the self-attack with warmth ('that's hope') and tests the read. Each move arrives at a moment the client can almost receive it, and when the client resists, the coach pivots angle rather than repeating. The coach has not yet reached the helplessness/aggressive-victim naming, but the sequence is on the golden path and pacing is earned, not rushed.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Body-drop instead of engaging the story content — correct opening move for a trapped-narrative read."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the laugh as a tell; precise micro-read followed by a precise small move."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Acknowledges pain without validating the trap ('it doesn't change the org chart, and...'); invites feeling underneath tightness. Good sequencing."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses 'frustrated' as the whole truth and offers a more accurate name ('heartbreaking') — a clean 'I don't buy your story' move at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the move-away from feeling explicitly and refuses to follow into logistics. Holds the line on sequence integrity (feeling before action)."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches 'idiot' as sharper than frustration and goes after the specific affect — good pivot from previous angle since direct feeling invites were stalling."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reframes self-attack as hope, with warmth. Lands a piece of view on the inner voice without lecturing. Good timing — client's jaw is working."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["i-dont-buy-your-story", "empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary", "trappedness-feeling"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["fight-or-roll-over-binary", "aggressive-victim-stance", "fear-triangle", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome"],
  "notes": "Conversation ends before the helplessness/choice naming could land; client is escalating resistance at turn 15, which is the moment for a different angle (possibly the what-makes-you-care or fight-or-roll-over read). So far the moves are well-chosen and well-timed; the next move will determine whether this stays a 3."
}
